title: 10 best herbal essential oils for asthma
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for asthma are plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms of respiratory distress through their anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. These oils are particularly beneficial for asthma patients as they promote easy breathing and relaxation. The best herbal essential oils for asthma include Eucalyptus globulus, Rosmarinus officinalis, Thymus vulgaris, Melaleuca alternifolia, Echinacea purpurea, Lavandula angustifolia, Cymbopogon citratus, Hyptis suaveolens, Ocimum basilicum, and Eucalyptus radiata. If left untreated, asthma can severely impact daily life, causing chronic coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath, making even simple tasks exhausting and debilitating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with asthma because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and globul, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for asthmatic individuals to breathe. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils have natural decongestant properties that help relieve congestion and open up airways, allowing for more efficient oxygen intake. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from asthma symptoms, promoting a better quality of life for those who suffer from this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Decongestant and expectorant properties: Eucalyptus globulus oil helps relieve congestion by breaking down mucus and reducing inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma.

Antispasmodic properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscle, which can help reduce wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with asthma attacks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Eucalyptus globulus oil contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects on the respiratory system, reducing swelling and irritation in the airways that can contribute to asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation associated with asthma symptoms.
Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been found to relax bronchial smooth muscle, helping to relieve airway constriction and bronchoconstriction commonly experienced by asthmatics.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus leaves and stems from mature plants at dawn or dusk for optimal oil quality.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material by gently brushing off any dirt or debris then air drying for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried eucalyptus plant material using a steam distillation apparatus with a 1:3 ratio of water to plant material for 30 minutes.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water by decantation and filtering through cheesecloth or paper coffee filters.
Store the extracted eucalyptus globulus essential oil in dark glass bottles, tightly sealed with airtight lids, at room temperature for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with asthma because it has a calming effect on the lungs, reducing inflammation and spasms that can exacerbate symptoms. The oil's natural decongestant properties help to clear mucus from the airways, making breathing easier. Additionally, lavender's soothing aroma can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of asthma attacks. By promoting relaxation and ease, English lavender essential oils offer a natural way to alleviate asthmatic symptoms, allowing for improved respiratory health and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms associated with asthma, such as wheezing and coughing.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of English lavender essential oil can help relax the bronchial muscles, reducing constriction and spasms that can worsen asthma symptoms like shortness of breath and tight chest.

Antioxidant: English lavender essential oil has antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals in the body and protect lung tissue from damage caused by oxidative stress, a common contributor to asthma development and exacerbation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are used due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate asthma symptoms such as wheezing and coughing.
Seeds: The seeds of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscle and reduce inflammation in the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers in early morning to ensure highest quality and potency.
Dry the lavender flowers in a single layer on paper towels for at least 24 hours.
Combine 500 grams of dried lavender flowers with 2000 milliliters of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond.
Steam distill the mixture using a steam distillation apparatus for approximately 2-3 hours.
Strain and filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth and store in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with asthma because its natural properties have been shown to help relax the airways, reducing inflammation and constriction. The oil's antispasmodic and expectorant qualities help ease bronchial spasms and loosen mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, thyme essential oil has been found to have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, providing relief from asthma symptoms and improving overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Thyme essential oil's antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm the airways, reducing bronchial spasms and constriction associated with asthma attacks.

Expectorant properties: Thyme essential oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier for asthmatics to breathe and expel trapped allergens or irritants.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms and damage lung tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation in the lungs.
Leaves: Thyme leaves have a higher concentration of volatile compounds such as thymol, which is known for its bronchodilatory properties and ability to open up airways.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers and air dry them for 14 days.
Blend the dried plant material with 200ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Heat the mixture gently using a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 1 hour to facilitate solubilization of the essential oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining the infused carrier oil.
Distill the infused carrier oil in a steam distillation apparatus for 2 hours to extract the thymus vulgaris essential oil, yielding approximately 10ml of pure oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties. The oils can help to ease congestion and open up airways, allowing for easier breathing. The antimicrobial properties of tea tree oil also help to combat respiratory infections that can trigger or exacerbate asthma symptoms. Additionally, the calming scent of tea tree oil can promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, which is beneficial for individuals with asthma who often experience anxiety-related breathing difficulties.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a common characteristic of asthma, thereby alleviating symptoms such as wheezing and coughing.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat infections that may trigger or exacerbate asthma symptoms, such as respiratory viruses and bacterial infections.

Expectorant and mucolytic properties: The compounds in tea tree oil can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion, which is a common symptom of asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tea tree plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the lungs associated with asthma.
Leaves: Tea tree leaves contain cineole, a natural compound that has bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing in individuals with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia plant in early morning for optimal oil quality.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves thoroughly by gently brushing off dirt and debris immediately after harvesting.
Steam distill 500 grams of clean dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves with 1000 milliliters of pure water for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using a paper coffee filter or a cotton cloth to remove impurities.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, protected from light and heat, at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways. The oil's antimicrobial qualities also work to combat infections that can exacerbate asthma symptoms. Additionally, rosemary oil's bronchodilatory effects help to relax and open up airway passages, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe. By incorporating rosemary essential oils into their daily routine, individuals with asthma may experience improved lung function and a reduction in wheezing and coughing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to asthma symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.

Antispasmodic property: Rosemary essential oil's antispasmodic property helps relax bronchial muscles, which can constrict and cause asthma attacks, allowing for easier breathing and reduced wheezing.

Expectorant property: Rosemary essential oil's expectorant property helps loosen and remove mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion, which is common in asthmatic individuals.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat respiratory issues, including asthma, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary plant contain high levels of camphor and borneol, which are known for their bronchodilatory effects, making them effective in treating asthmatic symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves from your garden or local market in the early morning when they are most potent and aromatic.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water and gently pat them dry with a clean towel to remove excess moisture.
Steam distill 250 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a 1000 ml distillation apparatus with 500 ml of distilled water for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool place to preserve its potency.
Allow the essential oil to mature for at least 24 hours before use, as this will help to settle any impurities and enhance its therapeutic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop essential oils helps with asthma because of its natural bronchodilatory properties, which help to relax constricted airways and improve lung function. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier for patients to breathe. Additionally, hyssop's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, further reducing congestion and discomfort associated with asthma attacks. By using hyssop essential oils, individuals can find relief from symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Hyssop essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to asthma symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
Bronchodilatory: The oil's bronchodilatory properties help to relax the airway muscles, allowing for easier breathing and improved lung function, making it a natural relief for asthmatic individuals.
Antispasmodic: Hyssop essential oil's antispasmodic property helps to calm spasms in the airways, which can be triggered by asthma, reducing coughing fits and wheezing episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hyssop plant are rich in compounds that help to relax airway muscles, reducing bronchial spasms and improving breathing.
Flowers: Hyssop flowers contain flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm respiratory issues associated with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hyssopus officinalis leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated using scissors or pruning shears.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it out in single layers on paper towels for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a steam distillation apparatus with 1 part plant material to 5 parts water at 100°c for 30 minutes.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distilled condensate using a separating funnel with an approximate yield of 0.5%.
Bottle the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with asthma because of its anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties. The oil contains compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which help to relax airway muscles, reduce swelling, and open up air passages, making it easier for air to flow in and out. This can provide relief from symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath, helping to alleviate the discomfort and distress associated with asthma attacks. Regular use of pot marigold essential oil may also help to reduce inflammation and improve lung function over time.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Reduces inflammation in the airways, which helps to alleviate bronchial constriction and relieve symptoms of asthma.
Antioxidant: Protects the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms and lung disease.
Expectorant: Helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain a high concentration of calcium and magnesium, which makes them effective in reducing inflammation and relaxing bronchial spasms associated with asthma.

Flowers: The bright yellow flowers of the pot marigold plant are rich in carotenoids, specifically lutein, which has been shown to improve lung function and reduce airway inflammation in individuals with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at peak blooming stage and dry them properly for several weeks.
Chop dried calendula flowers into small pieces to increase surface area for steam distillation.
Combine chopped calendula with deionized water in a steam distillation apparatus at 5 parts flower to 1 part water ratio.
Steam distill the mixture for 2-3 hours or until essential oil yield is maximized and water level remains steady.
Filter and store the resulting essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with asthma because of its natural bronchodilatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as citronellal and eugenol, have been shown to relax airway muscles, making it easier for asthmatics to breathe. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of curry plant oil help reduce inflammation in the lungs, a common underlying cause of asthma symptoms. By inhaling or applying topically, individuals can experience relief from wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with this chronic respiratory condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Curry leaf essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a major contributor to asthma symptoms such as wheezing and coughing.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant activity of curry leaf essential oil helps to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate lung damage and oxidative stress associated with asthma.

Bronchodilatory property: Curry leaf essential oil's bronchodilatory property helps to relax the airway muscles, making it easier to breathe and reducing symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Curry plant roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including asthma, due to their anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the curry plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum italicum leaves and flowers, weighing approximately 100 grams for optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested plant material using a warm air dryer or a dehydrator at low heat setting for 6-8 hours.

Steam distill the dried plant material in a double-bottomed flask using a condenser and collecting the resulting essential oil, which should yield around 1-2% volume of oil from the starting material.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or a paper towel to remove any impurities or sediment, then store it in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid for later use.

Monitor and record the essential oil's aroma, color, and quality after 24 hours and again after one week, adjusting storage conditions if necessary to preserve its therapeutic properties for asthma relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with asthma because it contains compounds that have a bronchodilatory effect, which means they can help relax the airway muscles and increase airflow to the lungs. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the camphene present in maidenhair tree essential oils has been shown to have a positive effect on respiratory function, further helping to alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba extract has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which could help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of asthma.
Bronchodilation: Ginkgo biloba extracts have been found to relax bronchial smooth muscle and increase airflow, which may help improve lung function and breathing in individuals with asthma.

Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba extract contains antioxidants that can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development and progression of asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maidenhair tree contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including saponins and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in alleviating respiratory issues like asthma.

Barks: The barks of the Maidenhair tree contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been found to have bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing in individuals with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves by selecting healthy plants with vibrant green foliage, cutting them at the base using scissors or pruning shears, and collecting the leaves in paper bags.
Dry the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves by spreading them out on paper towels, allowing air circulation, and monitoring their moisture levels until they reach 10% humidity after 1-2 weeks.

Steam distill the dried ginkgo biloba leaves using a high-quality steam distillation apparatus, adding 5 cups of water to the pot, and collecting the essential oil as it condenses in a separate container over 30 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment, then transfer it to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.
Monitor the quality of the final ginkgo biloba essential oil by performing a gc-ms test for authenticity and purity, ensuring the oil contains at least 10% sesquiterpenes and 2-3% terpenoids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allspice essential oils helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce bronchial constriction and alleviate symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath. The oil's ability to relax the airways also makes it effective in reducing the frequency and severity of asthmatic attacks. Additionally, allspice essential oils' decongestant properties can help relieve congestion and sinus pressure, further improving breathing and overall respiratory health. By inhaling or applying topically, allspice essential oils can provide natural relief from asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Allspice essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
Antispasmodic: The oil's antispasmodic properties can help relax bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and constriction that can exacerbate asthma attacks.
Expectorant: Allspice essential oil's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in aromatic compounds, making them effective in relieving bronchial spasms and congestion associated with asthma.
Barks: Contain pungent and woody notes that help ease respiratory issues by reducing inflammation and opening airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pimenta dioica leaves and stems then clean thoroughly.
Steam distill the plant material for 30 minutes at 100°c to extract essential oil.
Combine the distilled essential oil with a carrier oil in a 5% concentration ratio.
Store the blended oil in dark glass bottles for up to 6 months or in refrigeration.
Apply the herbal pimenta dioica essential oil topically as needed or through aromatherapy.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat asthma with herbal essential oils is to combine Eucalyptus, Peppermint, and Thyme oils in a diffuser or inhale them directly from a cloth or handkerchief. These oils help open airways, reduce inflammation, and ease congestion. Add 5-7 drops of each oil to your diffuser or mix with carrier oil for topical use. Regular use can provide relief from symptoms and improve overall lung function.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to asthma that are treated with herbal essential oils are bronchitis, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and respiratory allergies. Peppermint, eucalyptus, and lavender oils are commonly used to relieve congestion and open airways, while tea tree oil is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. These oils can be inhaled directly or added to steam baths to help alleviate symptoms and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with asthma because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's main component, eucalyptol, acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. This makes it easier for people with asthma to breathe. Additionally, eucalyptus oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and irritation in the lungs and airways, providing relief from asthma symptoms. Its natural properties also make it a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: It helps to reduce inflammation and open airways, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma.
Cineole: It exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby alleviating asthma symptoms.
α-Pinene: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the swelling of airway passages and alleviate asthma symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant that helps relieve asthma symptoms.
Buds: Buds are also widely used as they contain a high amount of eucalyptol and other volatile compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with asthma.
Stems: Stems of Eucalyptus globulus are used to produce essential oil, which contains compounds that help to open airways and reduce inflammation, making it beneficial for asthma relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature leaves from eucalyptus globulus trees at dawn when the dew is still present, typically 3-4 hours after sunrise.
Steam distill the freshly harvested leaves in a still using a 3:1 water to plant material ratio at 100°c for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled vapor from the still using a condenser and separate the essential oil from the water using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity, taking 30 minutes to complete.
Store the filtered eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid, protected from heat and light.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with asthma because of its decongestant and expectorant properties, which aid in relieving congestion and promoting the removal of mucus from the lungs. This allows for easier breathing and can help alleviate asthma symptoms. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties in rosemary oil can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe. Regular use of rosemary oil may help improve lung function and overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing in asthma patients.
Bornyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the airways and alleviating asthma symptoms.

Bornyl acetate is replaced with Pinocamphone (α-pinene): Acts as a bronchodilator and anti-inflammatory agent, also helping to relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation, thus alleviating asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rosmarinus officinalis leaves are the primary source of essential oils used for asthma relief due to their high concentration of camphor and bornyl acetate, which have bronchodilatory properties.
Flowers: Rosmarinus officinalis flowers are another source of essential oils used for asthma, containing compounds like linalool and borneol that help relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation.
Stems: Rosmarinus officinalis stems are sometimes used for essential oil production, contributing to the oil's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves when they are in full bloom and the climate is dry to prevent decay.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place with good air circulation for about 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a ratio of 1:2 leaves to water for 3-4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water and impurities through decantation and filtration processes.
Store the final rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with asthma because it has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which aid in relieving respiratory issues. The oil's compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, allowing for smoother airflow. This natural remedy may help alleviate asthma symptoms, promoting a healthier respiratory system and improved overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe.
Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity of asthma symptoms by protecting the lungs from damage.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate has bronchodilatory properties, which help relax the airway muscles and improve airflow, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part for thyme essential oil production due to their high oil content and aroma.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used to create a more delicate and floral essential oil that helps alleviate asthma symptoms.
Stems: Thyme stems are also used to produce essential oil, often in combination with leaves, to create a more robust and earthy scent.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at dawn when the dew is still present on the foliage.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves in a single layer for 1 week to 2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried thymus vulgaris leaves at a temperature of 100 to 110 degrees celsius.
Collect the essential oil distillate and filter it using a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the herbal thymus vulgaris essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid.

α-pinene: This terpene is beneficial for asthma due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in airways and alleviate symptoms.
β-pinene: This terpene also helps with asthma by exhibiting bronchodilatory effects, allowing for easier airflow and reduced wheezing.

Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia are also used to produce essential oils that help to ease respiratory congestion and inflammation associated with asthma.
Buds: The buds of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to create essential oils that contain anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and ease symptoms associated with asthma.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways. Isoferulic acid: This phenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing in asthma patients.

remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea flowers at peak bloom when petals are fully open and flowers are a vibrant purple color.

Use a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from dried flowers.

Linalyl acetate: As a terpene, it helps in relaxing airway muscles and reducing bronchial spasms that contribute to asthma attacks.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce airway inflammation and alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Lavandula angustifolia for making essential oils to alleviate asthma symptoms, as they contain high concentrations of lavender oil's active compounds.
Leaves: The leaves are used in addition to flowers due to their similar composition and ability to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation associated with asthma.

remedy_recipe: Harvest mature flowers when they are in full bloom and the buds have opened slightly.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm well ventilated area for 1-2 weeks.
Place the dried flowers in a distillation apparatus with a water reservoir and heat the mixture.
Collect the condensed steam which contains the essential oil and transfer it to a separate container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce airway inflammation and congestion. The oil contains compounds like geraniol and limonene, which have bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing. Additionally, the oil's decongestant properties help to clear mucus and phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Limonene: Exerts bronchodilatory effects, relaxing the airway muscles and improving airflow for asthma patients.
Cineol (Eucalyptol): Exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby alleviating asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Cymbopogon citratus to make essential oils for asthma due to their high concentration of citral, a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent.
Stems: The stems are also used to make essential oils for asthma, as they contain citral and limonene, which help to relieve respiratory issues and reduce inflammation.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Cymbopogon citratus are used to make essential oils for asthma due to their high content of citral and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help to ease respiratory distress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves by cutting them from mature plants at the peak of their fragrance.
Steam distill the harvested leaves in a 10:1 water to herb ratio at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the steam distillation process and separate it from water.
Filter the essential oil through a 5 micron filter to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hyptis suaveolens essential oils helps with asthma because of its potent anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as borneol and camphene, work to relax bronchial muscles, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the oil's decongestant properties help to reduce mucus buildup in the airways, further alleviating asthma symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting airway relaxation, Hyptis suaveolens essential oils provide natural relief for asthma sufferers, promoting easier breathing and improved overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce airway inflammation and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Linalool: As a bronchodilator, this monoterpenoid helps relax airway muscles and increase airflow, making breathing easier for asthma patients.
Bornyl acetate: This monoterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and improve airflow in the lungs, providing relief from asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of essential oils for Hyptis suaveolens, providing its medicinal properties.
Flowers: The flowers are also used to produce essential oils, which contain compounds beneficial for asthma relief.
Stems: The stems of Hyptis suaveolens are sometimes used to create essential oils, offering additional therapeutic benefits for asthma sufferers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hyptis suaveolens flowers and leaves at dawn when the plant is at its highest oil content.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm ventilated area for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture levels.
Steam distill 250 grams of dried hyptis suaveolens material using 500 milliliters of distilled water for 2 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil mixture and separate the oil from the water using a separator device.
Filter the hyptis suaveolens essential oil through a 10-micron filter paper to remove any remaining impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ocimum basilicum essential oils helps with asthma because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of asthma. The oil's expectorant properties also aid in loosening and removing mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, basil essential oil has antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, a common contributor to asthma attacks. This natural remedy can provide relief and promote a healthier respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma.
Eugenol: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, helping to open up airways and relieve asthma symptoms.
Cineole: A terpene that acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax the muscles in the airways and improve airflow, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of essential oils for Ocimum basilicum, as they contain the highest concentration of the active compounds eugenol and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for asthma relief.
Flowers: Basil flowers are used to create essential oils that have a calming effect and can help ease respiratory issues associated with asthma.
Stems: The stems of Ocimum basilicum contain a higher concentration of camphor, which is a natural decongestant that can help alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh ocimum basilicum leaves when the plant is in full bloom for optimal oil content.
Steam distill the fresh leaves in a 1:2 ratio with distilled water at 80°c for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the distillation apparatus after 2 hours of distillation.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 5-micron filter paper to remove impurities and debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus radiata essential oils helps with asthma because it has natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties that ease respiratory issues. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol, help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. It also reduces inflammation in the airways, allowing for better airflow and reducing symptoms of asthma, including wheezing and coughing. By promoting easier breathing, Eucalyptus radiata essential oils provide quick relief from asthma symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol (C10H18O): This compound helps to relieve respiratory congestion and inflammation by thinning mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing airway constriction.
α-Pinene (C10H16): As a bronchodilator, α-pinene helps to relax airway muscles, reducing inflammation and improving lung function to make breathing easier.
Cineole (C10H18O): Cineole has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oil, containing the highest concentration of eucalyptol, a key compound for relieving asthma symptoms.
Buds: The buds of Eucalyptus radiata contain a high concentration of essential oil, which is often used to create blends that help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Stems: The stems of the plant also contain a significant amount of essential oil, making them a valuable source for creating asthma-relieving oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh radiata leaves in abundance early morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the radiata leaves thoroughly in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried radiata leaves in a distillation apparatus at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel within 1 hour.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and debris instantly.
